# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for edge computing security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS = new Set([
  "users",
  "products",
  "orders",
  "sessions",
]);

const ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS = new Set([
  "id",
  "status",
  "category",
  "type",
  "region",
]);

const MAX_KEY_DEPTH = 4;
const MAX_PARAM_LENGTH = 100;
const MAX_RESULTS = 100;

function sanitizeKeyPart(input: string): string {
  if (typeof input !== "string") {
    throw new Error("Invalid key part type");
  }
  
  if (input.length > MAX_PARAM_LENGTH) {
    throw new Error("Key part exceeds maximum length");
  }
  
  const sanitized = input.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, "");
  
  if (sanitized.length === 0) {
    throw new Error("Invalid key part after sanitization");
  }
  
  return sanitized;
}

function validateAndBuildKey(
  collection: string,
  params: Record<string, string>
): Deno.KvKey {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.has(collection)) {
    throw new Error("Invalid collection");
  }

  const keyParts: Deno.KvKeyPart[] = [collection];

  for (const [param, value] of Object.entries(params)) {
    if (!ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(param)) {
      throw new Error(`Invalid filter parameter: ${param}`);
    }

    if (keyParts.length >= MAX_KEY_DEPTH) {
      throw new Error("Maximum key depth exceeded");
    }

    const sanitizedValue = sanitizeKeyPart(value);
    keyParts.push(sanitizedValue);
  }

  return keyParts;
}

async function handleSearch(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const collection = url.searchParams.get("collection");

    if (!collection) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection parameter required" }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }

    const filterParams: Record<string, string> = {};
    for (const [key, value] of url.searchParams.entries()) {
      if (key !== "collection" && ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(key)) {
        filterParams[key] = value;
      }
    }

    const keyPrefix = validateAndBuildKey(collection, filterParams);

    const results: Array<{ key: Deno.KvKey; value: unknown }> = [];
    const entries = kv.list({ prefix: keyPrefix }, { limit: MAX_RESULTS });

    for await (const entry of entries) {
      results.push({
        key: entry.key,
        value: entry.value,
      });
    }

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        collection,
        filters: filterParams,
        count: results.length,
        results,
      }),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
        },
      }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: error.message || "Invalid request" }),
      { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
    );
  }
}

async function handleGet(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const collection = url.searchParams.get("collection");
    const id = url.searchParams.get("id");

    if (!collection || !id) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection and id parameters required" }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }

    const key = validateAndBuildKey(collection, { id });
    const result = await kv.get(key);

    if (result.value === null) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }), {
        status: 404,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        key: result.key,
        value: result.value,
        versionstamp: result.versionstamp,
      }),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
        },
      }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: error.message || "Invalid request" }),
      { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
    );
  }
}

async function handleSet(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const collection = url.searchParams.get("collection");
    const id = url.searchParams.get("id");

    if (!collection || !id) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection and id parameters required" }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }

    const body = await request.json();

    if (!body || typeof body !== "object") {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid body" }), {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    const key = validateAndBuildKey(collection, { id });
    const result = await kv.set(key, body);

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        success: true,
        key,
        versionstamp: result.versionstamp,
      }),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
        },
      }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: error.message || "Invalid request" }),
      { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
    );
  }
}

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);

  if (url.pathname === "/search" && request.method === "GET") {
    return handleSearch(request);
  }

  if (url.pathname === "/get" && request.method === "GET") {
    return handleGet(request);
  }

  if (url.pathname === "/set" && request.method === "POST") {
    return handleSet(request);
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }), {
    status: 404,
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  });
}

serve(handler);
